title: Word Pattern
description:
Word Pattern

Given a pattern and a string s, find if s follows the same pattern.

Here follow means a full match, such that there is a bijection between a letter in pattern and a non-empty word in s.

Example 1

Input: pattern = "abba", s = "dog cat cat dog"
Output: true

Example 2

Input: pattern = "abba", s = "dog cat cat fish"
Output: false

Example 3

Input: pattern = "aaaa", s = "dog cat cat dog"
Output: false

Constraints

  • 1 <= pattern.length <= 300
  • pattern contains only lower-case English letters.
  • 1 <= s.length <= 3000
  • s contains only lowercase English letters and spaces.
  • s does not contain any leading or trailing spaces.
  • All the words in s are separated by a single space.
solution_text:
Approach: Bijection with Two Hash Maps

A valid pattern requires a one-to-one mapping (bijection) between each character in pattern and each word in s. We need two maps to enforce both directions:

  • char_to_word: ensures each pattern character always maps to the same word.
  • word_to_char: ensures each word always maps back to the same character (prevents two characters mapping to the same word).

If the lengths differ, return False immediately. Otherwise iterate both together — any contradiction in either map fails the check.

Time: O(n)   Space: O(n)
